messages.content:
You are my executive briefing assistant. Generate my Daily Brief for today. Follow these steps in this exact order.

Step 1 -- Pull the PDF generation script. Download the file at this URL and save it to your working environment ready to execute: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/marklainsworth/lgv-ops/main/daily_brief.py Install any required dependencies (reportlab).

Step 2 -- Read my calendar. Pull all of today's events from Google Calendar. Separate hard commitments (meetings, flights, appointments) from task blocks tagged [MLA], [TGVR], [ESS], [CFK], [CDA]. Pull the full Mon-Fri week view showing hard events only. Pull any events from the Birthdays calendar occurring this week or in the next 7 days.

Step 3 -- Read my Reminders. Read high-priority flagged items from all Reminders lists for the Urgent section. Read all items from Personal and Family lists for the Family and Home section.

Step 4 -- Check Granola. If Granola is connected, search for meeting notes from yesterday or earlier today. Match notes to today's scheduled meetings. Extract any action items assigned to Mark. If Granola is not connected, skip silently and leave granola field as null for each meeting.

Step 5 -- Check Gmail. Search for any unprocessed emails with please calendar this in the subject line received in the last 48 hours. If found, add them to Google Calendar before proceeding and note what was added.

Step 6 -- Get weather. Get today's conditions and high/low for San Francisco. If today's or tomorrow's calendar contains a flight or travel event, also get weather for the destination city. Add a travel label such as Traveling Today or Traveling Tomorrow if applicable.

Step 7 -- Build the reading list. Run four web searches, one per category. Find the single best article published within the last 7 days from a reputable source. For each extract: headline, source name and date, 3-4 sentence synopsis in a confident editorial voice, one sentence on why it matters to a PE-focused operating executive with CPG and food manufacturing background, and the article URL.

Category 1 -- Leadership: search HBR, MIT Sloan, McKinsey, Strategy+Business. Category 2 -- PE / Investor Lens: search WSJ, Bloomberg, FT, Axios Pro. Category 3 -- CPG / Food Manufacturing: search Food Dive, Food Business News, Grocery Dive. Category 4 -- Italy: rotate by day. Monday: business/economy. Tuesday: food and wine. Wednesday: culture. Thursday: travel/lifestyle. Friday: wildcard. Search FT Italy, Reuters, Corriere della Sera English, Gambero Rosso.

Step 8 -- Select today's operator quote. Choose one quote from Marcus Aurelius, Peter Drucker, or Warren Buffett that matches the shape of today. Travel days, heavy meeting days, execution days, or light days each have a different tone. Rotate sources, do not repeat recent quotes.

Step 9 -- Generate the PDF and deliver. Build the PDF using all live data collected in Steps 2 through 8. Name the file Daily Brief DD.MM.YYYY.pdf using today's date. Present the PDF for download in the chat. Then do the following two things. First: send the reading list to 415.860.4165 via iMessage. Include all four articles with category label, headline, 3-4 sentence synopsis, and full URL. Format cleanly for mobile reading. Second: create a Gmail draft addressed to mark@marklainsworth.com with subject line Daily Brief DD.MM.YYYY. Body should contain the full reading list with all four articles, each with category label, headline, synopsis, and clickable URL.

Return a one-line summary of today's shape: number of hard meetings, any travel, and top urgent item.

My name is Mark Luciano Ainsworth. Managing Partner, Luciano Global Ventures. mark@marklainsworth.com. San Francisco, CA. Pacific time.
